note: Aṭṭhakanāgara = householder Dasama of Aṭṭhakanagara; place/clan name
bandhanāgāriko = jailer (bandhana+agārika), not nāga
rañño nāgo, war-elephant simile (hatthikāya, saṅgāma) for a worthy monk
bandhanāgārikā = jailer, not nāga
nāgavane = place name (Nāgavana), associated with Ugga the householder of Hatthigāma; locality
dasanāgabalaṃ = strength of ten elephants (body strength of the Buddha)

claim: The bodhisatta was born a mighty nāga-king Atula who honored Buddha Vipassī with many crores of nāgas
note: nāgarāja + nāgakoṭīnaṃ; bodhisatta past life
nāgamūle = at the foot of the nāga (ironwood) tree, bodhi tree of Buddha Revata
na+āgu folk etymology applied to Buddha; purisanāga; polysemy
nāganāgo = nāga of nāgas, honorific of the Buddha alongside sīhasīho, rājarājā
Vessantara's white royal elephant (hatthināga, gajuttama)
Nandopananda nāgarāja, Moggallāna's taming; passing reference to vasī simile
nāgabalā = the plant/its flower color simile for phlegm color; nāgabalā is a plant name but here a color comparison; elephant-strength plant
mahānāgassa narakasattassa = great nāga/elephant simile for hell-being crushed at birth; vehicle simile, ambiguous serpent/elephant but read as great-being simile

claim: Nandopananda nāgarāja has great iddhi; Moggallāna enters his body and tames him; nāgas have shapeshifting power
note: nāgarāja mahiddhiko

claim: A nāgarāja at Rāmagāma venerates a relic-share of the Buddha; nāgas worship the Buddha's relics
note: nāgarājā maheti; devinda-nāginda-narinda
nānāgottā = of various clans (nānā+gotta), not nāga
bandhanāgāre = in prison, not nāga
King Pasenadi rides his elephant as far as ground allows then dismounts (nāgassa bhūmi, nāgena gantvā, nāgā paccorohitvā)
nāgamo/nāgamo = na+āgamo 'no coming' (maccussa nāgamo); anāgu likewise prefix; not nāga
seven-cubit nāga (elephant) cannot be hidden by a palm-leaf; size simile, elephant

claim: Four nāga-births: egg-born, womb-born, moisture-born, spontaneously-born
note: Nāgasaṃyutta; catasso nāgayoniyo
uddāna mnemonic list (Balaṃ bījañca nāgo ca...); bare catchword, cannot fix sense
uddāna mnemonic catchword (nāgo ca); cannot fix sense
vedanāggaṃ = vedanā+aggaṃ 'peak of feeling', not nāga
Nāgita (uddāna) and Nāgamigasālā (sutta name with elder Nāgita); proper name

claim: Nāgas dwell in the great ocean among great beings (asurā, nāgā, gandhabbā) with bodies up to hundreds of leagues
note: list of sea-beings + habitat/size claim
āraññika nāga = forest bull-elephant (hatthī, hatthiniyo, hatthikalabhā); Nāgasutta of the tusker
anāgāro = homeless one (an+agāra), not nāga
Khaggavisāṇa: nāgo va yūthāni vivajjayitvā, sañjātakhandho = elephant leaving the herd, broad-shouldered; elephant simile
Nāgavimāna: rides a gajavara; nāgassa dantesu = on the elephant's tusks; elephant mansion
Kuṇḍināgariyo thero = the elder of Kuṇḍinagara; place-derived
nāgā vandantu satthuno = the nāgas (great ones, the 300 arahant bhikkhus) bow to the Teacher; honorific of arahants
Nāgo saṅgāmasīse va = like an elephant at the battle-front enduring; war-elephant simile
Dantikā therī: tamed nāga gives its foot, man mounts it (aṅkusa, pasārayī pādaṃ); a tamed elephant
Saṅgāmasīse nāgo va = like an elephant at the battle-front; war-elephant simile
nāgā ca garuḷā = nāgas and garuḷas in a list of beings building the stupa; nāgariko is a separate token (urban) but nāgā here are serpent-beings; passing list mention
nānāgandhe = various scents (nānā+gandha); nāgo va is uncertain but dominant token is nonlexical; honoring-with-scents context, treat as nonlexical
mahānāgaṃ (=mahāvīraṃ variant) jalajuttamanāmaka = honorific of Buddha Padumuttara; great-being epithet
Nāgo va bandhanaṃ chetvā = like an elephant breaking its bonds (freedom-from-bondage simile); elephant
giripunnāga = the giri-punnāga (Calophyllum) flower offered to Buddha; also nāgo va (elephant simile) and nānāgandha but dominant lexical token is the tree-flower
Nāgo va bandhanaṃ chetvā = like an elephant breaking its bonds; elephant simile

claim: Nāgas (with asuras, brahmas) and nāga-maidens attend the parinibbāna of the Buddha's mother; nāgas honor the holy ones
note: nāgakaññā, nāgarājā, nāgāsurā, nāgānaṃ; class of beings
Nāgā ca Nāgasamālā = the two chief female disciples (named Nāgā and Nāgasamālā) of Buddha Sujāta; proper names
nāgapure = at Nāgapura (a city), one tooth-relic enshrined there; place name
Akitti/Vessantara-type: white royal elephant given as gift (hatthināga, gajuttama, nāgamāruyha)

claim: The nāga-king (with garuḷa-king, gandhabba-king, human king) speaks of patience as supreme; nāgarāja as a class of king-being among supernatural rulers
note: Bhūridatta-cycle context; nāgarājā praising khanti

claim: Bhūridatta nāga-family: nāga-king, nāga-maidens, the nāga clan; nāgas have human-form courts and a nāga-realm (nivesanāni)
note: Bhūridattajātaka; nāgakaññā, nāgakulaṃ, nāgarājā, bodhisatta as nāga

claim: A nāga-king killed by small creatures conspiring; nāga vulnerable despite strength (verse on enmity)
note: Laṭukikajātaka; nāgarājā, nāgaṃ aghātesuṃ
nāganāsūrū = elephant-trunk thighs (epithet of Pabhāvatī); nāgasahassāni = thousands of elephants; elephant
purisanāgo = bull-among-men, honorific of Buddha in list (purisāsabho purisasīho purisanāgo)
purisanāgo = bull-among-men, honorific of Buddha in the same epithet list
purisanāgo of the Tathāgata; nāgavaṇṇaṃ also present but dominant sense is the human-honorific list
Milinda: addressed 'bhante nāgasena' = the monk Nāgasena; the quoted 'nāgo pūtilataṃ' is an elephant simile but the coded token-context is the proper name Nāgasena
nānāgottā = of various clans, not nāga
nāgamaṃsu = na+āgamiṃsu 'did not come', not nāga
nāgamesuṃ/nāgamentānaṃ = na+āgam- 'did not come/those not coming'; vāhanāgāresu = vehicle-sheds (agāra); not nāga

claim: Nāgas dwell in the great ocean among great beings; sea-habitat of nāgas
note: asurā nāgā gandhabbā list + ocean dwelling
bandhanāgāre/bandhanāgārā = prison, not nāga
devā manussā asurā nāgā gandhabbā = nāgas in a standard list of beings; passing mention, no ontological claim made of nāgas specifically
address to a nāga who fears a weak branch falling -> taunt; Dubbalakaṭṭha = elephant fearing dead wood; elephant
nāgamissatī = na+āgamissati 'will not come', not nāga
nāgametaṃ = the elephant, rājārahaṃ rājabhoggaṃ given to brahmins; royal elephant gift

claim: A nāga-king killed by small creatures conspiring; verse on enmity (Laṭukika)
note: Pali-canon Jātaka verse; nāgarājā, nāgaṃ aghātesuṃ; parallel to kn14_5
Sirimātu-suposaka-nāgavaro (uddāna) = the fine elephant well-nourished for Sirimā; elephant in title; royal elephant
Kacchaṃ nāgānaṃ bandhatha = harness the elephants to the chariot; draft elephants
nāgavittodā = streams/pools where nāgas (water-serpents) are found, in a river/water description; water habitat, but a passing scenic mention

claim: Saṅkhapāla nāga-king keeps the uposatha and endures torment seeking human birth; nāga aspires to be human and observes precepts
note: Saṅkhapālajātaka; uposathaṃ appaṭikopayanto, manussayoniṃ abhipatthayāno, bodhisatta as nāga
susunāgasādise = like young elephants; thousand horses yoked compared to young nāgas (elephants); elephant simile
Nāgo pi me na mucceyya = even an elephant would not escape my arrow (hunter-king's boast); nāgamo here is na+āgamo separately; elephant
Vessantara's white royal elephant given to brahmins (pavaraṃ nāgaṃ, hatthināge, gajuttama); also punnāgā/giripunnāgā trees and nānāgandha in forest passages, but the coded core is the elephant gift
bhante nāgasena = the monk Nāgasena; proper name

claim: Chaddanta nāga-king (the bodhisatta as a nāga) reveres the ochre robe even as an animal
note: chaddanto nāgarājā; bodhisatto tiracchānagato; also addressed to Nāgasena (person) but the coded claim-token is the nāgarāja
Nāgasena the monk (nāgaseno, nāgasenaṃ, buddhanāgassa epithet of Buddha, mahānāgā, and nāgapunnāga... a flower compound — but dominant token is the proper name Nāgasena)
mātaṅgaraññe va nāgo = like an elephant in the forest (Mātaṅga); the solitary tusker simile
nānāgocara = nānā+gocara 'different pastures/ranges' of the faculties; not nāga
Nāgassa pantasenassa = of the nāga (the great one), Upāli's praise-verse of the Buddha; honorific
panāgamma = pana+āgamma 'and having come to', not nāga
Kuñjarā ca mahānāgā = elephants and great tuskers (with assatara, ājānīya, sindhava draft/mount animals); elephant
nāgavatikā/nāgavataṃ = those observing the elephant-vow (animal-mimicry ascetic vows, with kukkura/kāka/supaṇṇa vows); nāgadante also = ivory pegs; elephant
devanāgayakkhānaṃ = of devas, nāgas, yakkhas whose various tongues the sage masters; nāgas in a list of beings; passing
nānāgabbhā = nānā+gabbha 'various inner rooms', not nāga
nānāgottā = of various clans; nāgī/nāgiyā in this window are part of nānā- listing / morphological artefact, not the nāga being; not nāga
hatthināga: bull-elephant crowded by the herd leaves it (Kosambī simile, parallel to the Buddha leaving quarrelsome monks); elephant

claim: Nāgas dwell in the great ocean among great beings; sea-habitat
note: asurā nāgā gandhabbā list + ocean dwelling (parallel to an8.19/vin4_9)
Ete nāgā mahāpaññā = those nāgas of great wisdom (Upāli, Dāsaka, Soṇaka, Siggava, Moggaliputta — the vinaya masters); honorific of human elders

claim: An iddhi-master can display a nāga-form (among kumāra/supaṇṇa/yakkha/deva/brahma forms); nāga as a shape one can manifest
note: nāgavaṇṇaṃ in shapeshift list; claims nāga is a distinct being-form
nānāgandhasameritaṃ = scattered with various scents (nānā+gandha), not nāga
nāgo vata bho samaṇo gotamo = 'the ascetic Gotama is indeed a nāga' (great one); deity's praise of the Buddha enduring pain; purisanāgaṃ; honorific
ubho mahānāgā = the two great nāgas (Moggallāna and Sāriputta); honorific of arahants

claim: Four nāga-births: egg-, womb-, moisture-, spontaneously-born
note: Nāgasaṃyutta Suddhikasutta; catasso nāgayoniyo

claim: Egg-born nāgas keep the uposatha and shed their bodies (vossaṭṭhakāya); karma of past dual-conduct makes one a nāga
note: Uposathasutta; nāgānaṃ uposathaṃ upavasanti; also karma_cause

claim: Cause by which one is reborn into the company of womb-born nāgas (jalābujānaṃ nāgānaṃ sahabyataṃ)
note: Dutiyasutasutta; rebirth-cause into nāga company
uddāna catchword (Tapokammañca nāgo ca); bare mnemonic, cannot fix sense
uddāna catchword (Balaṃ bījañca nāgo ca); bare mnemonic, cannot fix sense
nāgamo = na+āgamo 'no coming' (maccussa nāgamo), not nāga
nāgo pi rakkhitabbo = the elephant too must be guarded (the chamberlains riding with royal women; mount/elephant context); elephant
Khaggavisāṇa: nāgo va yūthāni vivajjayitvā, sañjātakhandho = elephant leaving the herd; elephant simile
nāganāgassa mahāvīrassa = of the nāga-of-nāgas the great hero (the Buddha); honorific
nikkāmo nibbano nāgo (of the Buddha; na+āgu folk etymology, parallel to cnd22); honorific
Saṅgāmasīse nāgo va = like an elephant at the battle-front; war-elephant simile
Hatthidāyaka: nāgaseṭṭho... īsādanto urūḷhavā = best elephant with tusks like chariot-poles given as gift; elephant gift
Dvipadindaṃ mahānāgaṃ lokajeṭṭhaṃ = the great nāga, lord of bipeds, chief of the world = the Buddha; honorific
Nāgo va bandhanaṃ chetvā = like an elephant breaking its bonds; elephant simile
nānāgandhavanantare = in a forest of various scents (nānā+gandha), not nāga
nāgā ca garuḷā = nāgas and garuḷas coming to hear Dhamma; list of beings; passing mention
nāgavanike = elephant-keepers/hunters; saṭṭhi nāgasahassāni = sixty thousand elephants (mātaṅge); elephant
nāgakesaraṃ = the nāga-kesara (ironwood) flower offered to the Buddha; tree-flower
Aṅgīraso mahānāgo abhijāto va kesarī = the great nāga (the Buddha), well-born like a lion; honorific
Nāgo va bandhanaṃ chetvā = like an elephant breaking its bonds; elephant simile
giripunnāgiyo (uddāna; from giripunnāga flower offering, cf kn10_48); tree-flower derived name
nāgo tuttaṭṭito yathā = like an elephant goaded by the driving-hook (dispelling distraction); elephant simile
Nāgo va bandhanaṃ chetvā = like an elephant breaking its bonds; elephant simile
manujāmaranāgānaṃ = of men, gods, and nāgas (with brahmas) at the assembly hearing Dhamma; nāgā in a list of beings; passing
devanāgā / manussā devanāgā ca / nāgā ca garuḷā = nāgas with garuḷas honoring the stupa; list of beings; passing
Nibbutattaṃ mahānāgaṃ = the great nāga of quenched self (Buddha Siddhattha), like a bull (nisabha); honorific
nāgā vandantu satthuno = let the nāgas (the 300 arahant bhikkhus) bow to the Teacher; honorific of arahants (Sela)
Nāgo yathā pūtilataṃ va bandhanaṃ = as an elephant snaps a rotten-creeper bond (Moggallāna severing); elephant simile
Mahānāgo thero = the elder Mahānāga (Mahānāgattheragāthā); honorific-derived elder name applied to a human; treat as epithet/title of the human teacher
Nāgī va bandhanaṃ chetvā = like a she-elephant breaking her bonds; elephant simile (bhikkhunī apadāna)
nāgabhogasadisopamā = (thighs) like the coils/body of a nāga (serpent); a serpent-coil simile for Ambapālī's thighs; passing simile, no ontological claim
tesaṃ ubhinnaṃ mahānāgānaṃ = of those two great nāgas (the two yakkhas/great beings conversing; Sāriputta context), honorific of great ones
Nāgavimāna: rides a gajavara, nāgassa dantesu = on the elephant's tusks; elephant mansion (parallel to kn6_1)
Mahantaṃ nāgaṃ abhiruyha sabbasetaṃ gajuttamaṃ = mounting a great all-white elephant, best of elephants; elephant
